summaryText: Guest Speaker Chris Durso opened the first service of 2023 with a powerful call to spiritual maturity, anchored in 2 Timothy 2:15. He contrasted two types of believers: the 'scavenger,' who collects spiritual words from various sources but lacks a deep, personal foundation, and the 'student,' who diligently studies Scripture and applies it. Pastor Chris explained that scavengers often skip the source (God Himself), don't allow the Holy Spirit to settle in them, and ultimately drown in life's storms because their faith is based on second-hand experiences. In contrast, students study the Word, allow the Word to study them, and learn to stand firm in storms by wielding Scripture as their weapon. Using the stories of Peter walking on water and Jesus being tempted in the wilderness, he illustrated the necessity of keeping our eyes on Jesus and fighting spiritual battles with God's written Word. The message culminated in a passionate declaration that Jesus Himself is the ultimate Word we need for every situation in 2023 and beyond, encouraging the congregation to move from being passive consumers to active, disciplined students of God's truth.
mainPoints: Scavengers rely on second-hand spiritual experiences and drown in storms, while students go directly to God's Word as their source.
A student of God studies Scripture to be prepared for life's tests and allows the Holy Spirit to apply the Word to their life.
We stand in life's storms by fighting with the 'it is written' authority of Scripture, just as Jesus did when tempted.

versesMentioned.text: Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.

This is what we speak, not in words taught us by human wisdom but in words taught by the Spirit, explaining spiritual realities with Spirit-taught words. The person without the Spirit does not accept the things that come from the Spirit of God but considers them foolishness, and cannot understand them because they are discerned only through the Spirit.

But when you ask, you must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind. That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Such a person is double-minded and unstable in all they do.

The tempter came to him and said, "If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread." Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.'"

Monday.scripture: 2 Timothy 2:15 (NIV) - Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who does not need to be ashamed and who correctly handles the word of truth.
Monday.focus: Committing to be a student, not a scavenger.
Monday.action: Set aside 15 minutes today to read your Bible without any other media or commentary.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me a hunger for Your Word directly from You. Help me to be a faithful student.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 2:13 (NIV) - This is what we speak, not in words taught us by human wisdom but in words taught by the Spirit, explaining spiritual realities with Spirit-taught words.
Tuesday.focus: Relying on the Holy Spirit as our teacher.
Tuesday.action: As you read Scripture today, pause (Selah) after one verse and ask the Holy Spirit to reveal its truth to you.
Tuesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, settle in me. Teach me spiritual realities and help me understand Your Word.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 14:29-31 (NIV) - "Come," he said. Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus. But when he saw the wind, he was afraid and, beginning to sink, cried out, "Lord, save me!"
Wednesday.focus: Keeping our eyes on Jesus in the storm.
Wednesday.action: Identify one worry or 'storm' you are facing. Write down a promise from God's Word that speaks to that situation.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, when I am afraid, help me to fix my eyes on You and trust Your word over my circumstances.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 4:4 (NIV) - Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.'"
Thursday.action: Memorize one verse this week that addresses a common area of struggle or temptation in your life.
Thursday.prayer: God, Your Word is my sustenance and my weapon. Help me to hide it in my heart so I can stand firm.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 139:13-14 (NIV) - For you created my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother’s womb. I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well.
Friday.focus: Allowing the Word to study us and affirm our identity.
Friday.action: Read a passage about God's love and creation (like Psalm 139). Reflect on what it says about who you are in Christ.
Friday.prayer: Father, show me through Your Word who You say I am. Heal any lies I have believed about my identity.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 8:37-39 (NIV) - No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us. For I am convinced that neither death nor life... will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
Saturday.focus: Standing on God's Word as our unshakable foundation.
Saturday.action: Review the verses and truths you've engaged with this week. Write a short declaration of faith based on them.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I am convinced of Your love and power. I declare that I am more than a conqueror through Christ and will stand on Your Word.
